children.name: p
children.textContent: A city archivist discovers that surfaces hold emotional imprints from everyone who's touched them. Cracked pavement carries grief; old glass holds longing. When she reads the city like a book, she finds the oldest textures carry a message addressed specifically to her.

children.name: span
children.textContent: "the way light hits old glass differently in winter"

children.name: span
children.textContent: "rust as a feature, not a flaw"
